item.title: Japan rules out major domestic risks from private credit for now
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Friday private credit does not ​pose a major issue domestically at present but noted ‌risks linked to the $2 trillion industry could be discussed at next week's G7 finance meeting.

item.title: Chinese startup ShengShu raises $293 million to advance artificial general intelligence
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence startup ShengShu Technology has raised 2 billion yuan ($292.59 million) in a funding round led by Alibaba Cloud, ​the company said on Friday, as competition intensifies in ‌China's AI sector.

item.title: Dollar set for weekly drop ahead of US-Iran peace talks
item.description: The dollar on Friday was heading for its largest weekly drop since January as other currencies gained on optimism that a ceasefire in the Gulf will hold and oil shipping will resume.

item.title: Stocks shaky as Israeli attacks on Lebanon tests Iran ceasefire
item.description: Asian stocks ticked up early on Friday but gains ​were capped as traders questioned the durability of this week's U.S.-Iran ceasefire and remained wary of fragile hopes for ‌Israel-Lebanon peace talks.

item.title: Oil prices rise after strikes on Saudi oil facilities
item.description: Oil prices rose in early trading on Friday following attacks on Saudi energy infrastructure, and as markets evaluated the risk premium from the ongoing closure of ​the Strait of Hormuz, despite a fragile truce agreed between the U.S. and ‌Iran.

item.title: SpaceX posted nearly $5 billion loss in 2025, The Information reports
item.description: Elon Musk's IPO-bound SpaceX posted a loss ​of nearly $5 billion in 2025 ‌on revenue of more than $18.5 billion, The Information reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Bessent, Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic model risks, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott ​Bessent and Federal ‌Reserve Chair Jerome Powell convened an urgent ​meeting with ​bank executives on Tuesday to warn ⁠them about the ​cyber risks raised ​by Anthropic's latest AI model, Bloomberg News reported, ​citing sources.

item.title: Apple to shutter its first unionized US store in Maryland
item.description: Apple said on Thursday that it will shut down its retail store in Towson, Maryland, ​the first of its U.S. locations where retail ‌employees successfully unionized in 2022.

item.title: Indonesia sends letter of reprimand to YouTube over breach of social media curbs, minister says
item.description: Indonesia has sent Google a letter of reprimand because its YouTube platform has not ​complied with new social media curbs for children, ‌a senior minister said, the first such sanction since the rules took effect last month.

item.title: Meta pulls ads aimed at recruiting plaintiffs for social media addiction lawsuits
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Thursday it is pulling ads from Facebook and Instagram aimed at recruiting new plaintiffs ​for ongoing litigation accusing it and other social media companies of designing their platforms to be addictive to young users.

item.title: Exclusive: Anthropic weighs building it own AI chips, sources say
item.description: Artificial intelligence lab Anthropic is exploring the possibility of designing its own chips, ​three sources said, as the company and its rivals respond to ‌a shortage of AI chips needed to power and develop more advanced AI systems.

item.title: Fitch cuts FS KKR Capital rating to junk territory as asset quality weakens
item.description: Fitch Ratings on Thursday downgraded its rating on a private credit fund jointly run by Future Standard and KKR ​to junk territory, citing continued deterioration in its asset quality.

item.title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law
item.description: xAI filed a lawsuit on Thursday seeking to block Colorado from enforcing a new law regulating artificial intelligence systems, escalating a fight over whether ​oversight should be handled by states or by Washington.

item.title: Meta transfers top engineers into new AI tooling team
item.description: Meta is drafting top software engineers from across the company into a new AI engineering organization created last month, according to an internal memo seen by ​Reuters, part of a reorganization of its workforce in preparation for layoffs.

item.title: Ecuador raises tariffs on Colombia to 100% from 50%
item.description: Ecuador's government on Thursday said it was raising tariffs ​on imports from its larger neighbor Colombia to ‌100% from a prior level of 50%, citing Colombia's alleged failure to implement border security measures.

item.title: White House warned staff not to place market bets amid Iran war, WSJ reports
item.description: The White House warned U.S. ​government staff against improperly ‌leveraging their positions to place bets in ​futures markets ​in an email on March ⁠23, the ​Wall Street Journal reported ​on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Trump says Iran should not charge fees to tankers going through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump said on Thursday Iran should not ​charge fees to tankers going through the Strait of Hormuz, which has ‌been blockaded since the start of the Iran war, causing the worst disruption to global energy supplies in history.

item.title: CarMax settles with activist Starboard with two new board appointments
item.description: CarMax said on Thursday it would add Bill Cobb and Jim Kessler to its ​board in connection with its ‌2026 annual shareholder meeting, after activist investor Starboard Value pushed for changes ​at the used-car retailer.

item.title: TRADING DAY Ceasefire sends stocks higher
item.description: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq clocking their seventh daily gain, as investors shrugged off a rise in oil prices and pinned their hopes on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire extending to Israel and Lebanon too.

item.title: US considers new crackdown on Chinese telecom companies, FCC says
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications ​Commission said it may ‌bar three major Chinese telecom companies ​from operating data ​centers in the United ⁠States and ban ​other carriers from ​connecting with them in the United States.

item.title: USTR's Greer sees no changes in data rules that bar Chinese vehicles from US
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Thursday that President Donald Trump's administration does not plan any changes ‌to a crackdown on vehicle hardware and software from China, which effectively bars Chinese cars from the U.S. market.

item.title: StubHub to pay $10 million to settle FTC ticket price case
item.description: StubHub Holdings on Thursday agreed to pay $10 million to settle U.S. Federal Trade Commission ​charges that the platform failed to disclose ‌total ticket price for live events to consumers.

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike stamp prices to 82 cents
item.description: The cash-strapped U.S. Postal Service said on Thursday it ​wants to raise the price of first-class mail stamps to ‌82 cents from 78 cents effective July 12.

item.title: US Treasury yield forecasts creep up, but strategists cling to benign inflation view
item.description: U.S. Treasury yield forecasts are only a bit higher than a month ago despite the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, according to market strategists polled by Reuters who are still putting off making major changes to their longer-term inflation view.

item.title: CFO Anthony Armstrong leaves xAI, the Information reports
item.description: Anthony Armstrong, named xAI's CFO in October, has departed the company as part of a ​broader wave of senior exits, the Information ‌reported on Thursday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Jack Daniel's owner, source says
item.description: Privately held U.S. drinks maker Sazerac ​is exploring a deal for Brown‑Forman, according to a source familiar with ‌the matter, potentially emerging as a rival suitor for the Jack Daniel’s maker that is already in talks with France’s Pernod Ricard over a possible merger.

item.title: Iran war raises demand for US fuel, boosting Gulf Coast refining margins
item.description: U.S. Gulf Coast refiners are reaping the strongest margins in years as disruptions to Middle Eastern oil flows from the Iran war raise demand for U.S. fuel exports, ​analysts and experts said.

item.title: Berkshire unit must face brokerage commission lawsuit despite $250 million settlement, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge ​ruled on Thursday that a Berkshire Hathaway unit must face a ‌proposed antitrust class action claiming it conspired to inflate commissions that home sellers pay real estate agents, even though its HomeServices of America brokerage settled the same claims for $250 ​million in 2024.

item.title: Paramount seals debt commitments backing Warner Bros deal
item.description: Paramount Skydance has syndicated its bridge loan facility and secured permanent financing from a group of ​banks for its planned $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros ‌Discovery .

item.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
item.description: Tesla investors said ​the company's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV, which was reported exclusively by Reuters, might boost ‌volumes and address slowing demand, but at the cost of already strained margins.
